page: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It includes an L3 cache of 480 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration, while network connectivity is established at a baseline of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU scoring 417,683.29 and PassMark CPU scoring 180,710.06. Memory bandwidth is highly rated, reaching 30,918.01 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 1,148.36 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a top-tier Redis SET throughput of 531.59 million operations per second and strong PassMark database scores. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the middle 50% tier. Additionally, while LLM prompt processing is strong, text generation speeds are average to poor, particularly for smaller 135M models which fall into the bottom 10% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare-metal instance offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, trading off local storage for massive DDR5 memory capacity and high-speed 100 Gbps network throughput. The absence of integrated GPUs makes it less suitable for active model training, but its advanced matrix extensions (AMX) support efficient CPU-based inference. The server is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, heavy multi-threaded compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 384 vCPUs across 192 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB per core, alongside a 480 MB L3 cache and a 100 Gbps baseline network speed. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional database throughput, with Redis SET operations reaching 531.59 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds for small models represent notable performance tradeoffs. This server is suited for in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs, 3072 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for in-memory databases and heavy workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring massive DDR5 capacity and high-bandwidth networking for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores
3072 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Suited for in-memory databases, heavy compilation, and web serving
